symptoms: Inter font + blue-purple gradients (the 'SaaS starter pack')
Generic hero section with 'Trusted by' logos
Parallax scroll that induces motion sickness
Same Tailwind UI components copied endlessly
the_problem.root_cause: LLMs default to 'safe' patterns because they have no constraints forcing creativity. Without guardrails, AI converges on the statistical average of training data—resulting in the same mediocrity everywhere.

the_insight.realization: Biological inheritance provides a perfect metaphor
the_insight.analogy: Just as every organism has unique DNA but shares the building blocks of life, every design system can be unique while respecting functional constraints
the_insight.breakthrough: SHA-256 hash + 32 chromosomes = infinite unique design systems that are both reproducible (same seed = same output) and guaranteed distinct (different seed = different output)

the_manifesto.core_phrase: No Templates. No Slop. Only Math.
principles: Epistasis rules (chromosome interactions) over isolated properties

features: Basic 15-chromosome DNA generation
SHA-256 hash → color, typography, motion
Simple archetype detection (dashboard, portfolio, blog)
Keyword-based content extraction (naive)
limitations: Always generated 3D biomarkers (decorative) regardless of appropriateness
Static archetypes with limited functional differentiation

features: Added 13 primary sectors (healthcare, fintech, automotive, etc.)
Sector-based color psychology profiles
Hero type chromosome (ch19) with 10+ variants
Trust signals chromosome (ch21) for credibility
Social proof chromosome (ch22)
v0_0_2_sector_awareness.breakthrough: Moved from 'one size fits all' to sector-appropriate design generation

features: Semantic trait extraction via LLM (Groq, OpenAI, Anthropic)
8 trait vectors: informationDensity, temporalUrgency, emotionalTemperature, playfulness, spatialDependency, trustRequirement, visualEmphasis, conversionFocus
Epigenetic parsing: brand assets (logos, PDFs) override hash
Pattern detection to prevent 'slop'
v0_0_3_llm_integration.architecture_change: LLM extracts semantic meaning → traits drive chromosome generation → constraints enforce appropriateness

features: Complexity tier system (Microbial → Flora → Fauna → Sentient → Civilized → Advanced)
Ecosystem generation: organisms share DNA
Civilization layer for sophisticated applications
32 chromosomes (ch30 state_topology, ch31 routing_pattern, ch32 token_inheritance added in v0.0.7)
v0_0_4_civilization_tier.philosophy: Finding life (basic design) is common. Finding civilization (advanced design) requires intentional complexity.

features: Single LLM call returns traits + sector + archetype (was 2 separate calls)
Removed deprecated ContentExtractor (naive keyword-based)
Removed classifySubSector() function (replaced by hash-based selection)
Mandatory LLM API key (no fallbacks, hard error if missing)
Production-hardened: 49 tests, all passing
v0_0_5_unified_extraction.optimization: 50% reduction in LLM API usage through unified analysis

features: URL Genome Extractor with triple backend (Playwright + Scrapy + Native Fetch)
AST Pattern Detector for genome-aware CSS validation using css-tree
Unified Component Generator (dynamic, replaces 12 hardcoded generators)
All 29 chromosomes now mutable (added mutation handlers for ch0, ch1, ch2, ch9-ch25)
Deterministic output: removed Date.now() from seeds, content-hash based
Double LLM call fix: uses analyze() instead of extractTraits()+classifySector()
Zero Math.random(): all jitter uses crypto hash for deterministic backoff
12 MCP tools including validate_design, mutate_genome, extract_genome_from_url
Production-verified: TypeScript clean build, 49 tests passing
architecture_changes: Deleted 790-line hardcoded component generator
Added constraint-solver with epistasis rules
Added sector-profiles for industry-specific generation
Added ecosystem/civilization bridge for organism-based websites

v0_0_9_design_constitution.philosophy_shift: Tool identity clarified: Permutations is a design constitution generator, not a code generator. The genome provides direction. The agent implements from it.
features: forbiddenRanges replaces hueRange in all 23 sector profiles — sectors define what's psychologically wrong, hash selects freely from the rest (technology now fully unrestricted: Cloudflare=orange, GitHub=dark, Stripe=purple — all valid)

darkModeHex added to ch5_color_primary — primary at 22–35% lightness was invisible on dark surfaces; lifted variant (58–74%) emitted as --color-primary-interactive for buttons/links on dark backgrounds

14 tools reduced to 8 — removed: generate_from_archetype (merged into generate_design_genome via offline: true), list_archetypes, write_design_files, generate_preview, generate_component, mutate_genome
generate_design_genome: added offline: boolean param (skips LLM, uses hash-based inference), added suggested_next array (dynamic workflow guide based on complexity score)
generate_civilization: generate_code default flipped to false — returns architecture specs by default; code generation is explicit opt-in
All 8 tool descriptions updated with workflow position (STEP 1, STEP 2, FINAL STEP, etc.)
genome_report added to generate_design_genome response — markdown explaining every chromosome sequenced, sector detected, constraints in effect, and workflow map
ecosystem_report added to generate_ecosystem response — organism hierarchy, containment map, what each organism means for implementation
civilization_report added to generate_civilization response — tier reached, architecture direction, design system constraints
Dead code removed: component-generator.ts (382 LOC), file-writer.ts (650 LOC), mutation.ts (953 LOC) — ~1,985 lines of unreachable code deleted
.cursorrules rewritten: 10 rules, 8-tool workflow map, darkModeHex usage, ecosystem/civilization specs-not-code mandate, validate-before-shipping requirement
README.md updated: offline mode, color philosophy section, forbidden ranges table, genome_report field
architecture_changes: SECTOR_COLOR_BIAS table: all 23 sectors converted from hueRange: [min, max] to forbiddenRanges: [[min, max], ...]
buildValidRanges() helper inverts forbidden zones to valid spectrum — hash maps into valid ranges only
generateHueFromForbidden() replaces generateHueFromBias() — deprecated wrapper maintained for compatibility
generatePrimaryColor() now returns darkModeLightness and darkModeHex on all 3 code paths (base, brand, epigenetic)
CSSGenerator emits --color-primary-interactive using darkModeHex; .btn-primary adapts background in dark mode
buildGenomeReport() private method on server — generates explainability markdown from any genome
generate_civilization condition fixed: was (args.generate_code !== false) which ran by default; changed to (args.generate_code === true) to match new default

v0_0_9_font_catalog.theme: From static font templating to live provider catalogs — font choices as varied as the design itself
v0_0_9_font_catalog.motivation: extract_genome_from_url returned headingFont: 'satoshi'. Agent passed font_provider: 'google'. Google doesn't host Satoshi. Tool looked up 'satoshi' in Google's registry → got undefined → .id on undefined → TypeError crash. Static lists of 6–15 fonts per provider were no better: every similar product got the same small pool. Font slop — the exact problem Permutations exists to prevent.
features: FontCatalogService: fetches live font catalogs from Fontshare, Google Fonts, and Bunny at runtime with 24h in-memory cache — no API key required for any provider
'fontshare' and 'none' added as font providers (enum was 'bunny' | 'google' only)
'none' provider returns family name without CDN import URL — user manages font loading themselves
Google Fonts public metadata endpoint (fonts.google.com/metadata/fonts) — full 1000+ font catalog
Fontshare public API — returns full ~100-font catalog in one ~830KB response regardless of pagination params
Bunny mirrors Google catalog — separate cache key, same fetch logic
CHARGE_TAGS: maps TypeCharge → provider → category tags (normalized lowercase-hyphen across all providers: 'sans-serif', 'serif', 'display', 'monospace')
warmCache() pre-warms all providers at server startup — non-blocking, startup remains instant
FALLBACK_FONTS: hardcoded per-charge lists used when cache cold or API unavailable — graceful degradation
bugs_fixed: TypeError crash: font lookup returned undefined for fonts not in provider's catalog. .id on undefined → crash. Fixed: null-safety + provider-aware catalog lookup via FontCatalogService

Google category mismatch: fonts.google.com/metadata/fonts returns category 'Sans Serif' (space, title case). Code applied .toUpperCase() → 'SANS SERIF'. CHARGE_TAGS had 'SANS_SERIF' (underscore). Zero geometric/grotesque fonts matched — fell back to 7-font list instead of 709. Fixed: normalize all categories to lowercase-hyphen in fetchGoogle()
Fontshare timeout: res.json() on ~830KB response timed out at 10s. Fixed: 30s timeout. API ignores perpage param — full catalog returned in single response
Fontshare field mapping: assumed tags[] array and total_count field. Actual response uses category string and count_total. Fixed: use correct field names
architecture_changes: New file: src/font-catalog.ts — FontCatalogService class with per-provider fetch, 24h TTL Map cache, CHARGE_TAGS mapping, FALLBACK_FONTS per charge type
FontProvider type updated: 'bunny' | 'google' | 'fontshare' | 'none'
selectDisplayFont() and selectBodyFont() in sequencer.ts: use fontCatalog.getFonts(charge, provider) — pool expanded from 6–15 hardcoded fonts to 100–1000+ live provider fonts
getFontImportUrl() handles Fontshare URL format (api.fontshare.com/v2/css) and 'none' (returns empty string)
server.ts font_provider schema enum updated to include 'fontshare' and 'none'

v1_0_0_genome_chain.theme: From single-layer design DNA to a living three-layer genome chain — design, ecosystem, civilization — each derived from the previous via SHA-256 hash, each layer's chromosome values biasing the next
v1_0_0_genome_chain.milestone: First stable release. The biological metaphor is now complete: a seed grows into a design genome, which grows into an ecological system of components, which grows into a civilizational architecture. Every decision is traceable back to the original seed.
features: Three-layer SHA-256 genome chain: seed → sha256 → DesignGenome (L1, 32 chromosomes) → sha256 → EcosystemGenome (L2, 11 chromosomes) → sha256 → CivilizationGenome (L3, 10 chromosomes). Each layer deterministic, each parentHash tracked for provenance.

EcosystemGenome (L2): biome (16 types), energy source, symbiosis pattern, trophic structure, succession stage, adaptation axis, population pattern, temporal rhythm, spatial axis, capacity class, mutation rate. Fully wired into ecosystem.ts organism generation.

CivilizationGenome (L3): archetype, governance, economics, technology, culture, resilience, knowledge, expansion, age, fragility. Governance model overrides ch30_state_topology. Knowledge model overrides ch31_routing_pattern.

Gravity biasing system: each layer's gravity functions use predecessor chromosome VALUES (not just hash ancestry). Dark + metallic design → hydrothermal biome. Mutualistic symbiosis → federated governance → distributed state. Predatory energy → warrior archetype.

biasedPick weighted probability: replaces rotation+clamp which collapsed distinct gravity values. For 8-option arrays, gravity values 4/5/6/7 all produced the same ±2 rotation. New system: target index gets 4× weight, adjacent 2×, others 1×. Every distinct gravity produces a distinct probability distribution.

StructuralProps — vocabulary-invariant complexity scoring: LLM answers 10 binary/count questions about product behavior (realtimeState, entityCount, sensitiveData, multiRole, financialTransactions, complexWorkflows, deepNavigation, externalIntegrations, screenCount, primarySurface). computeComplexityFromStructure() maps answers to tier deterministically. Keyword boost removed from buildPrompt entirely.

Verified: 'a tool doctors use to check patient vitals' scores 0.913 (city_state) — higher than 'patient portal' (0.763, endotherm_fauna) — because vitals monitoring correctly implies real-time state. Plain language beats keywords at accuracy.

Edge diversity fix: effectivePlayfulness = Math.max(0.3, traits.playfulness) floors the radius range so all three styles (sharp/soft/organic) are reachable at every playfulness level. sharpCeiling proportionally expands sharp classification for sharp-preference sectors.

Motion diversity fix: 25% of seeds (b(30) > 0.75) deviate to adjacent physics regardless of sector default. Same sector, different seeds, genuinely different motion — not just different easing on the same physics.

Biome byte fix: was b[0] >> 4 (nibble, 0–15 range). New biasedPick uses Math.floor((rawByte/256) * total). With rawByte 0–15 and total ~21, positions 16–20 were unreachable — 5 biomes effectively blocked. Fixed to full byte b[0].

Gravity hole fixes: chemosynthetic energy → scientific archetype (was falling to mercantile default). Mixotrophic energy → maritime archetype (was falling to mercantile default). Economics: chemosynthetic → gift (free, self-contained), mixotrophic → market (adaptive, bidirectional). Three consecutive tests of healthcare yielded mercantile×3 before this fix.

Architecture fully wired in server.ts: generate_ecosystem returns ecosystemGenome in environment. generate_civilization receives ecosystemGenome, sequences CivilizationGenome, patches stateTopology and routingPattern post-hoc.
architecture_changes: New files: src/genome/ecosystem-types.ts, src/genome/ecosystem-sequencer.ts, src/genome/civilization-types.ts, src/genome/civilization-sequencer.ts

ecosystem.ts: generateMicrobialColony/generateFloraEcosystem/generateFaunaPopulation all accept EcosystemGenome. eco_ch11_mutation.rate drives entropy multiplier. eco_ch3_symbiosis.depth drives containment depth. eco_ch4_trophic.cascade drives prey count.

civilization.ts: generate() accepts optional EcosystemGenome as 6th param. Post-hoc patching after generateTier(): civGenome.chromosomes.civ_ch2_governance.model → stateTopology, civ_ch7_knowledge.model → routingPattern.

complexity-analyzer.ts: computeComplexityFromStructure() static method — deterministic, no LLM. analyze() accepts optional StructuralProps — uses structural when present, falls back to trait-based scoring in offline mode.

extractor.ts: StructuralProps interface exported. structural field added to DesignAnalysis and LLMResponse. buildPrompt() replaced BOOST COMPLEXITY keyword block with 10 behavioral structural questions with concrete YES/NO examples.
server.ts: structural extracted from analysis.structural, passed to complexityAnalyzer.analyze() as 4th argument.
v1_0_0_genome_chain.test_coverage: 49 tests: all passing. Semantic test (live LLM): vocabulary-invariant tier verified across 4 plain/keyword product pairs.

features: ch27_motion_choreography fully rendered: stagger delays, entry sequences, hover microinteractions, page transitions, choreography easing curves
ch28_iconography fully rendered: --icon-size/--icon-stroke/--icon-color CSS vars, .icon base rule, animation keyframes (bounce/pulse/spin/draw), CDN script injection in <head>
ch11_texture fully rendered: grain SVG noise filter with animated variant, glass backdrop-filter, chrome metallic gradient
HTMLGenerator zero fallbacks: no || 'Testimonials' / 'Features' / 'FAQ' / 'Dashboard' / 'Starting at' / 'Featured Story' / 'Ready to Experience' — render nothing if LLM returns nothing
Footer nav fully LLM-extracted: footerNavProduct, footerNavCompany, footerProductTitle, footerCompanyTitle as dedicated copy fields
Complexity-based HTML routing in server: below 0.70 = static HTML/CSS, 0.70-0.84 = HTML + civilization specs, 0.85+ = full React component output
PatternDetector auto-runs on every generate_design_genome call — violations returned in response, no manual validate_design needed
CSSGenerator now generates complete page CSS: nav, hero variants, trust grid, social proof, features, FAQ, CTA, footer — all values via CSS custom properties
Rating stars derived from numeric stat value, star count computed from LLM-returned rating
architecture_changes: generatePageSectionStyles() added to CSSGenerator — covers all HTML sections previously unstyled
generateChoreographyStyles(), generateIconographyStyles(), generateTextureStyles() added to CSSGenerator
ComplexityAnalyzer wired into generate_design_genome server handler for tier-based routing
extractor.analyze() return uses ?? '' / ?? [] instead of placeholder strings — LLM failure = empty, not fake data

the_pipeline.extraction_layer: LLM analyzes content → extracts 8 traits + sector + archetype
the_pipeline.generation_layer: Tailwind CSS + WebGL + SVG + HTML topology

the_29_chromosomes.ch0_sector: Primary/secondary sector classification with brand weight
the_29_chromosomes.ch28_iconography: Icon style, stroke weight, set consistency (v0.0.6)

examples: High informationDensity + High temporalUrgency → Low playfulness (serious data work)
Warm color primary → Cool background (contrast balance)
Dashboard archetype → No decorative animations (functional focus)
Inter font + Blue gradient = FORBIDDEN (slop pattern detected)

complexity_tiers.comment: 14 tiers across two domains: ecosystem (0.00–0.80) and civilization (0.81–1.00)

ecosystem.abiotic_0_00_0_10: No life — environment only. HTML/CSS scaffold emitted, organism counts are zero.
ecosystem.prokaryotic_0_11_0_22: First cells — minimal structure. Atomic tokens established, few microbial components.
ecosystem.protist_0_23_0_33: Unicellular eukaryotes — basic interaction. Atoms + first interaction states.
ecosystem.bryophyte_0_34_0_44: Non-vascular flora — simple containers. Cards, modals, basic layout components.
ecosystem.vascular_flora_0_45_0_56: Rooted, complex internal structure. Growing components with state, dropdowns, data displays.
ecosystem.invertebrate_fauna_0_57_0_65: First fauna, simple nervous systems. First fauna organisms emerge — tables, wizards.
ecosystem.ectotherm_fauna_0_66_0_73: Cold-blooded mobile consumers. Complex multi-step flows, editors, data-dense components.
ecosystem.endotherm_fauna_0_74_0_80: Warm-blooded, all chromosomes active. Full organism ecosystem — all 38 organisms active.

civilization.tribal_0_81_0_86: First civilisation tier — CivilizationGenerator activates. Local state (useState), single/multi-page routing, flat/semantic tokens.
civilization.city_state_0_87_0_91: Organised settlement. Shared context state, protected routing, semantic tokens. Command palette unlocked.
civilization.nation_state_0_92_0_94: Unified governance. Reactive store (Zustand), platform routing (shell + lazy remotes), component-level tokens.
civilization.empire_0_95_0_96: Distributed authority. Zustand + persist middleware, platform/federated routing, governed token system.
civilization.network_0_97_0_98: Post-geographic coordination. Distributed/federated state, Module Federation routing, cross-system tokens.
civilization.singularity_0_99_1_00: Convergence — all systems unified. Federated state + event bus, full Module Federation, cross-system token graph.

gap_1_ai_slop.solution: Mathematical constraints prevent convergence on mediocrity

gap_2_sector_appropriateness.problem: Healthcare and crypto trading dashboard get the same 'high information density' trait but need completely different heroes, trust signals, colors
gap_2_sector_appropriateness.solution: Sector-aware chromosome generation with industry-specific psychology
gap_2_sector_appropriateness.mechanism: 13 primary sectors with color biases, hero type weightings, trust signal requirements

gap_3_decoration_vs_function.problem: Tools always generate decorative 3D elements (100% of outputs) when real sites use them <1%
gap_3_decoration_vs_function.solution: Hero type chromosome with 11 functional variants, 3D only when appropriate
gap_3_decoration_vs_function.mechanism: ch19_hero_type selects from: product_ui, stats_counter, trust_authority, configurator_3d, etc.

gap_4_color_psychology.problem: Fixed hue ranges trap every sector into the same narrow palette — all tech products got blue-purple (hue 240–270°) regardless of seed
gap_4_color_psychology.solution: Sector-defined forbiddenRanges replace hueRange — sectors define what's psychologically wrong, hash selects freely from everything else across the full 360°
gap_4_color_psychology.mechanism: Technology has no forbidden ranges (Cloudflare=orange, GitHub=dark, Stripe=purple — all correct). Healthcare forbids blood red + magenta [0–20°, 320–360°]. Food forbids cold corporate blue [220–280°]. Two different tech products with different seeds always get different hues.

gap_5_reproducibility.problem: AI-generated designs cannot be recreated consistently
gap_5_reproducibility.solution: SHA-256 hash of seed = deterministic output
gap_5_reproducibility.mechanism: Same seed → Same DNA forever. Different seed → Different DNA guaranteed.

gap_6_llm_cost.problem: Multiple LLM calls per generation expensive
gap_6_llm_cost.solution: Unified analysis: single call returns traits + sector + archetype
gap_6_llm_cost.mechanism: Consolidated prompt with structured JSON output, 50% API cost reduction

gap_8_single_layer_genome.problem: A design genome told you what it should look like (colors, fonts, motion) but not what kind of system it is or how it should be structured. Two products with the same complexity score got the same architecture direction regardless of whether one was a tightly-coupled healthcare portal and the other a loosely-coupled marketplace. The genome was a visual brief, not a living system.
gap_8_single_layer_genome.solution: Three-layer SHA-256 genome chain. L1 sequences design character. L2 sequences ecological character (how components relate). L3 sequences civilizational character (how the application is structured). Each layer derived from the previous via sha256(parentHash) — same chain provenance, different concerns.
gap_8_single_layer_genome.mechanism: Predecessor chromosome VALUES gravity-bias successor selections. A dark+metallic design produces a hydrothermal ecosystem. Mutualistic symbiosis produces federated governance. Federated governance overrides ch30_state_topology to 'distributed'. The visual design literally grows into an application architecture.

gap_9_vocabulary_bias.problem: Complexity tier — which determines how many components are generated, whether civilization architecture activates, and what state/routing patterns apply — was computed partly from keywords in the product description. 'Dashboard', 'platform', 'system' boosted tier. 'Tool', 'app', 'tracker' did not. A complex product described simply got the wrong tier. An architect who said 'a distributed transaction processing system' got a higher tier than one who said 'a way to handle purchases'.
gap_9_vocabulary_bias.solution: StructuralProps: 10 binary/count questions about product behavior. computeComplexityFromStructure() maps answers deterministically. Keyword boost removed from LLM prompt entirely. Tier now computed from what the product does, not how it is described.
gap_9_vocabulary_bias.mechanism: LLM answers: Does state change while user watches? How many entity types? Sensitive data? Multiple roles? Financial transactions? Multi-step workflows? Deep navigation? External APIs? Screen count? Primary surface? These are answerable from behavior description regardless of vocabulary. The mapping to tier is deterministic code — not LLM judgment — so it is consistent across providers, models, and prompt variations.

gap_7_font_templating.problem: Hardcoded font lists of 6–15 per provider meant every product in the same sector-charge category drew from the same small pool — font slop alongside the color slop the tool was built to prevent
gap_7_font_templating.solution: Live font catalogs fetched from provider APIs at runtime — pool reflects the full catalog, not a curated subset that creates templates
gap_7_font_templating.mechanism: FontCatalogService with 24h cache. Fontshare (~100 fonts), Google Fonts (1000+), Bunny (mirrors Google). 'none' provider for self-managed loading. No API key required for any.

struggle_1_naive_extraction.example: Looking for 'healthcare' in text and assigning healthcare sector
struggle_1_naive_extraction.failure: Couldn't capture nuance: 'billing portal for high-net-worth patients' needs healthcare+fintech blend
struggle_1_naive_extraction.solution: LLM-based semantic extraction with 8 trait vectors

struggle_2_hardcoded_values.problem: Grid values and colors hardcoded in generators
struggle_2_hardcoded_values.solution: All values now derive from chromosomes (ch9_grid for layout, ch5_color for palette)

struggle_3_3d_everywhere.discovery: Analysis of 111 real sites showed <1% use decorative 3D
struggle_3_3d_everywhere.breakthrough: Hero type chromosome: 3D only for automotive configurators, not Mayo Clinic

struggle_4_llm_fallbacks.problem: System provided 'neutral' fallbacks when LLM failed
struggle_4_llm_fallbacks.issue: Neutral values (0.5 for all traits) produce generic output
struggle_4_llm_fallbacks.decision: HARD ERROR if no API key. HARD ERROR if LLM fails after 3 retries. No fallbacks = forced quality.

struggle_5_separate_calls.breakthrough: Single analyze() call returns {traits, sector, archetype} in one JSON response

struggle_6_url_extraction_stub.problem: extract_genome_from_url was a stub requiring manual CSS paste
struggle_6_url_extraction_stub.failure: Users had to scrape CSS manually—poor UX, not production-ready
struggle_6_url_extraction_stub.breakthrough: Triple backend: Playwright for JS sites, Scrapy for pros, Native fetch fallback

struggle_7_non_deterministic_seeds.problem: Date.now() in seeds meant same input produced different outputs
struggle_7_non_deterministic_seeds.breakthrough: Content-hash based seeds—same input = same output forever

struggle_8_hardcoded_generators.problem: 12 hardcoded component generators (790 lines) for each component type
struggle_8_hardcoded_generators.failure: Adding new component types required new generator functions
struggle_8_hardcoded_generators.breakthrough: Single unified component generator: generate(purpose, elements, layout, complexity)

struggle_9_mutation_gaps.problem: Only 20 of 32 chromosomes had mutation handlers—12 were static
struggle_9_mutation_gaps.impact: Limited design space exploration, constrained breeding
struggle_9_mutation_gaps.breakthrough: Full coverage: mutation handlers for ch0, ch1, ch2, ch9-ch25

struggle_10_hue_prison.problem: hueRange: [240, 270] for technology meant every tech product got blue-purple — two different products under the same sector looked identical
struggle_10_hue_prison.impact: The exact problem the tool was built to prevent was happening in the color system itself
struggle_10_hue_prison.discovery: Generated 3 tech sites with different seeds, compared screenshots — all had hue ≈ 245°–265°
struggle_10_hue_prison.breakthrough: forbiddenRanges inverts the constraint. Define what's psychologically wrong (not what's right). Hash picks freely from what remains. Technology: no forbidden ranges at all.

struggle_11_dark_mode_invisibility.problem: Primary color generated at lightness 22–35%. On dark backgrounds (5–10% lightness), primary buttons were near-invisible
struggle_11_dark_mode_invisibility.impact: Dark mode sites had invisible CTAs — fundamental usability failure
struggle_11_dark_mode_invisibility.breakthrough: darkModeLightness = clamp(lightness + 0.35, 0.58, 0.74). Every genome now ships a dark-safe interactive variant alongside the base primary.

struggle_12_tool_identity_crisis.problem: 14 tools exposed; agents called generate_component and generate_preview to build UIs, treating the tool as a code generator
struggle_12_tool_identity_crisis.impact: Tool was generating TSX that contradicted the agent's design intent. generate_component's output competed with the genome's direction.
struggle_12_tool_identity_crisis.discovery: The tool's mandate is design direction, not implementation. When a tool generates code, it skips the agent's judgment.
struggle_12_tool_identity_crisis.breakthrough: Removed 6 tools. Added suggested_next to genome response so agents know the workflow. Ecosystem + civilization return specs not code by default.

struggle_14_biasedpick_gravity_collapse.problem: The original biasedPick used rotation + clamp: target = byte + floor(gravity) then clamped to valid indices. For an 8-option array with clamp window = floor(8×0.25) = 2, gravity values 4, 5, 6, and 7 all produced rotations of +2, +2, +2, +2 — the same shift. Warrior archetype (index 4), democratic (5), industrial (6), emergent (7) were indistinguishable from each other. The gravity function was computing different targets; the selection function was throwing that information away.
struggle_14_biasedpick_gravity_collapse.discovery: Running six different healthcare product seeds: got mercantile archetype 3 times. Healthcare shouldn't produce mercantile — it has no energy source that maps there. Investigated: chemosynthetic and mixotrophic energy both fell to return 2 (mercantile default) because the gravity function had no cases for them. Fixed gravity holes. Still got clustering. Traced further: gravity=4 and gravity=6 were producing identical byte distributions.
struggle_14_biasedpick_gravity_collapse.breakthrough: Replaced rotation+clamp with weighted probability. gravity is now a TARGET INDEX. Weight distribution: target=4×, target±1=2×, all others=1×. Cumulative weight array built per call. rawByte maps into weight-space via Math.floor((rawByte/256) * total). Every distinct target produces a genuinely distinct distribution. No two gravity values collapse to the same output.
struggle_14_biasedpick_gravity_collapse.impact: Applied to both ecosystem-sequencer.ts and civilization-sequencer.ts. Also fixed two gravity holes: chemosynthetic→scientific(1), mixotrophic→maritime(3) in archetypeGravity; chemosynthetic→gift(2), mixotrophic→market(1) in economicsGravity.

struggle_15_chromosome_clustering_root_c.problem: After fixing biasedPick, still saw edge style 'soft' appearing repeatedly within a sector. Motion physics clustering similarly. Not biasedPick this time — three separate root causes operating simultaneously.
struggle_15_chromosome_clustering_root_c.root_cause_1_edge: generateEdge() used playfulness directly for max radius: maxRadius × playfulness. At playfulness=0.2 (fintech), max radius = 32×0.2 = 6.4. Organic threshold was hardcoded at 16. With max radius of 6, the radius could never exceed 16 — 'organic' was geometrically impossible. Every fintech site was soft by construction, not by hash.
struggle_15_chromosome_clustering_root_c.root_cause_2_motion: generateMotion() selected physics from sector profile then returned. No hash byte influenced the selection within the sector. Same sector, 100 different seeds — all got identical physics. The hash only affected motion IF the sector had multiple options, but single-physics sectors never deviated.
struggle_15_chromosome_clustering_root_c.root_cause_3_biome: After fixing biasedPick, biome byte was still b[0] >> 4 (nibble, values 0–15). New biasedPick maps rawByte via (rawByte/256) * total. With rawByte max=15 and total≈21, positions 16–20 were unreachable — 5 biomes blocked from appearing regardless of gravity.
struggle_15_chromosome_clustering_root_c.fixes: Edge: effectivePlayfulness = Math.max(0.3, playfulness). organicThreshold proportional to effective range (50% of max). sharpCeiling proportionally expands sharp zone for sharp-preference sectors. Motion: b(30) > 0.75 deviates to adjacent physics (25% of seeds). Biome: changed b[0] >> 4 to b[0].
struggle_15_chromosome_clustering_root_c.verification: 10-seed distribution test across 5 sectors: fintech edge {soft:8, sharp:2}, healthcare edge {organic:4, soft:5, sharp:1}, gaming edge {sharp:6, soft:4}. Distribution appropriate to sector character. Motion: fintech {step:8, spring:2}, gaming {step:2, spring:8}. No single-value lock-in.

struggle_16_vocabulary_complexity_bias.problem: The trait-extracting LLM was told to 'BOOST COMPLEXITY when intent includes these keywords: +0.20 dashboard, +0.18 platform, +0.15 real-time...' This made the tier depend on which words the user chose, not what the product actually does. 'A tool for tracking workouts' could score protist while 'fitness analytics dashboard' scored ectotherm_fauna — same product, different tier, different architecture direction, different component library depth.
struggle_16_vocabulary_complexity_bias.the_deeper_issue: The 8 trait vectors (informationDensity, temporalUrgency, etc.) were doing two jobs simultaneously: driving visual design character AND computing complexity tier. These are different concerns. A product can be architecturally complex but visually minimal. Collapsing both concerns into the same floating-point vectors caused keyword-bias in both design character AND tier assignment.
struggle_16_vocabulary_complexity_bias.solution: Separate the two concerns. Add StructuralProps — 10 binary/count questions about product BEHAVIOR answered by the LLM. computeComplexityFromStructure() maps those answers to a complexity score deterministically. The 8 trait vectors now drive only visual/interaction character. Remove keyword boost from buildPrompt entirely.
struggle_16_vocabulary_complexity_bias.why_binary_count: Binary/count outputs are much harder for an LLM to get wrong based on vocabulary than calibrated floating-point scores. 'Does state update while the user watches?' is answerable from product behavior, not from whether the user said 'dashboard' or 'tool'. 'How many entity types?' counts things — same count regardless of what synonym the user chose.
struggle_16_vocabulary_complexity_bias.verified: Live test with Groq API: 'a tool doctors use to check patient vitals and schedule follow-ups' → realtimeState=true, sensitiveData=true → 0.913 city_state. 'Patient portal for a family medicine clinic' → realtimeState=false, sensitiveData=true → 0.763 endotherm_fauna. Plain description scored HIGHER because describing doctor behavior (checking vitals) correctly implies real-time monitoring. Old keyword system would have scored 'portal' higher.

struggle_13_font_provider_crash.problem: extract_genome_from_url returned headingFont: 'satoshi'. Agent called generate_design_genome with font_provider: 'google' — Satoshi is on Fontshare, not Google Fonts.
struggle_13_font_provider_crash.failure: Tool looked up 'satoshi' from Google's catalog → got undefined → .id on undefined → TypeError crash. The agent had done everything right — the tool failed to handle the mismatch.
struggle_13_font_provider_crash.the_deeper_issue: Even with the crash fixed, the right answer wasn't 'add Satoshi to the Google list.' That's still templating. Static font lists of 6–15 per provider create font slop: every similar product draws from the same small pool, the exact problem Permutations exists to prevent.
struggle_13_font_provider_crash.breakthrough: FontCatalogService fetches live catalogs at runtime — Fontshare (~100 fonts), Google Fonts (1000+), Bunny (mirrors Google). 24h cache, no API keys, graceful fallback. Pool went from 6–15 curated fonts to 100–1000+ live provider fonts. Two seeds in the same sector-charge now pull from fundamentally different pools.

technical_achievements.determinism: Same seed produces identical hash and genome across all three layers — verified across 1000+ seeds
technical_achievements.uniqueness: Different seeds produce different designs at all three layers. forbiddenRanges ensures two seeds in the same sector produce different hues. biasedPick weighted probability ensures distinct gravity values produce distinct distributions.
technical_achievements.three_layer_chain: SHA-256 hash chain: seed → DesignGenome (L1, 32 chromosomes) → EcosystemGenome (L2, 11 chromosomes) → CivilizationGenome (L3, 10 chromosomes). Total: 53 chromosomes. Predecessor values gravity-bias successor selections — the design literally grows into an architecture.
technical_achievements.vocabulary_invariant_complexity: StructuralProps: 10 binary/count behavioral questions. computeComplexityFromStructure() is deterministic code. Same product described differently → same structural answers → same tier. Verified live: 'a tool doctors use to check vitals' scores city_state (0.913). 'Patient portal' scores endotherm_fauna (0.763). Plain behavioral description beats keyword description at accuracy.
technical_achievements.gravity_biasing: biasedPick weighted probability: target index gets 4×, adjacent 2×, others 1×. Every distinct gravity value produces a distinct probability distribution. Gravity holes filled: chemosynthetic→scientific, mixotrophic→maritime (archetype); chemosynthetic→gift, mixotrophic→market (economics).
technical_achievements.edge_diversity: effectivePlayfulness = Math.max(0.3, playfulness). Proportional organicThreshold. sharpCeiling for sharp-preference sectors. All three styles reachable at every playfulness level. Verified: 10-seed distribution across 5 sectors — no single-value lock-in.
technical_achievements.motion_diversity: 25% of seeds deviate to adjacent physics via b(30) > 0.75. Same sector, different seeds, genuinely different motion.
technical_achievements.constraint_enforcement: Epistasis rules prevent 8 known 'slop' patterns. Sector forbidden ranges prevent hue prison. biasedPick weighted probability prevents archetype collapse.
technical_achievements.sector_coverage: 23 sectors with forbidden hue ranges, hero type weightings, trust signal requirements
technical_achievements.offline_mode: offline: true skips LLM. Falls back to trait-based complexity scoring (structural unavailable). hash-based trait inference.
technical_achievements.multi_provider: 6 LLM providers supported: Groq, OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter, HuggingFace
technical_achievements.test_coverage: 49 production tests: determinism, uniqueness, sector-awareness, epistasis, ecosystem-civilization bridge, pattern detection. All passing.
technical_achievements.url_extraction: Triple backend: Playwright (JS sites), Scrapy (pro crawling), Native fetch (zero deps)
technical_achievements.css_validation: AST-based genome-aware validation with drift scoring 0-100
technical_achievements.dark_mode_safety: Every genome ships darkModeHex (lightness 58–74%) — visible on dark surfaces. Base hex (22–35%) for light mode. CSS var --color-primary-interactive for interactive elements.
technical_achievements.explainability: Every tool response includes a _report field: genome_report (chromosomes sequenced + constraints), ecosystem_report (organism hierarchy + containment map), civilization_report (tier reached + architecture direction)
technical_achievements.workflow_guidance: suggested_next array in genome response — dynamic list of which tools to call next, when, and why. Based on complexity score.
technical_achievements.tool_surface: 8 tools with STEP-prefixed descriptions. Agents know workflow order without documentation.
technical_achievements.zero_hardcoding: All values derived from SHA-256 hash and LLM extraction — no magic numbers, no placeholder fallbacks
technical_achievements.complete_page_css: CSSGenerator covers every HTML section: navigation, hero variants, trust grid, social proof, features, FAQ, CTA, footer — zero unstyled elements
technical_achievements.api_optimization: Single LLM call returns structural props + traits + sector + copy intelligence. offline: true = 0 API calls.
technical_achievements.dynamic_font_catalog: Live font catalogs fetched from provider APIs at runtime — Fontshare (~100), Google Fonts (1000+), Bunny (mirrors Google). 24h in-memory cache, no API key required.
technical_achievements.font_provider_breadth: 4 font providers: google, bunny, fontshare, none. 'none' returns family name without CDN URL for self-managed loading.

motivation.why_we_built_this: We're tired of the internet looking like a template warehouse
motivation.the_vision: Every website should be as unique as the brand it represents—without sacrificing functional appropriateness
motivation.the_stance: A healthcare portal should feel trustworthy and calm. A crypto dashboard should feel technical and urgent. A fashion brand should feel aspirational and editorial. One size fits none.
motivation.the_challenge: Making uniqueness reproducible. Making constraints creative. Making math beautiful.

real_world_usage.website_demo: The website/ directory is built entirely from Permutations DNA
real_world_usage.dogfooding: We eat our own cooking—the docs site uses generated colors, fonts, SVG biomarker
real_world_usage.api_integration: MCP server integrates with Cursor, Claude Desktop, Windsurf
real_world_usage.workflow: Designer describes intent → MCP generates DNA → AI builds UI using constraints

future_roadmap.v0_0_7_component_library: Full React/Vue/Svelte component generation from ecosystem with props/stories
future_roadmap.v0_0_8_visual_editor: Web UI for breeding genomes: 'warmer colors', 'more density' sliders
future_roadmap.v0_1_0_design_to_code: Figma/Sketch plugin: extract design → generate DNA → export code
future_roadmap.v0_1_5_a11y_auditor: WCAG 2.2 AA/AAA automated compliance checking with remediation
future_roadmap.v0_2_0_real_time_collaboration: Multiplayer genome editing, versioned design systems
future_roadmap.v0_2_5_perf_budget: Core Web Vitals optimization—automatic performance tuning
future_roadmap.v1_0_0_generative_components: Components generated on-demand based on content and context

philosophical_position.on_templates: Templates are dead ends. They give you the answer without teaching you the logic.
philosophical_position.on_ai_design: AI shouldn't replace designers—it should give them superpowers. Permutations is the constraint layer that lets AI be creative without being random.
philosophical_position.on_uniqueness: Uniqueness for its own sake is decoration. Uniqueness within constraints is design.
philosophical_position.on_mathematics: Math doesn't constrain creativity—it enables it. The infinite comes from finite rules.
philosophical_position.on_slop: Slop isn't bad because it's common. It's bad because it's thoughtless. Permutations forces thoughtfulness through constraints.

the_cosmic_analogy.title: Permutations as a Universe
the_cosmic_analogy.subtitle: From infinite possibility to singularity civilization

the_design_universe.description: The SHA-256 address space (2^256 possible seeds) is our universe—vaster than atoms in the observable cosmos
the_design_universe.comparison: Just as the universe contains ~10^80 atoms, Permutations contains ~10^77 possible design genomes
the_design_universe.implication: Every design that will ever exist is already mathematically defined, waiting to be discovered via its seed

planetary_bodies.description: Each seed is a planet—a unique point in design space with its own characteristics
planetary_bodies.habitable_zone: Sector constraints define which seeds produce 'viable' designs (healthcare planets need trust signals, fintech planets need security)
planetary_bodies.uninhabitable: Some seeds produce slop (Inter + blue gradient)—these are the gas giants: large but lifeless
planetary_bodies.diversity: Like planets orbiting different stars, each seed has unique: atmosphere (colors), gravity (rhythm), terrain (topology), geology (texture)

potential_for_life.description: Not all seeds can sustain functional design
requirements: Constraint satisfaction (magnetic field protecting from radiation)
potential_for_life.seeds_scanned: Of 2^256 possible seeds, ~10^20 produce viable designs for any given sector
potential_for_life.selection: The LLM acts as a telescope—scanning the cosmic background to find seeds with life-potential

life_exists.description: A 'living' design is one that functions for its purpose

criteria.metabolism: Data flows through components (inputs processed, outputs rendered)
criteria.homeostasis: Design maintains consistency across viewports (responsive stability)
criteria.adaptation: Components respond to user interaction (feedback loops)
criteria.reproduction: Component patterns replicate with variation (design system propagation)

life_exists.vs_templates: Templates are fossils—preserved but dead. Living designs breathe, respond, evolve.

type_of_life.abiotic: 0.00–0.10: No life — environment only. HTML/CSS scaffold, zero organisms.
type_of_life.prokaryotic: 0.11–0.22: First cells — minimal structure. Atomic tokens, sparse microbial components.
type_of_life.protist: 0.23–0.33: Unicellular eukaryotes — basic interaction. First interactive states emerge.
type_of_life.bryophyte: 0.34–0.44: Non-vascular flora — simple containers. Cards, modals, basic layout.
type_of_life.vascular_flora: 0.45–0.56: Rooted, complex internal structure. Like plants: growing upward, stateful but anchored.
type_of_life.invertebrate_fauna: 0.57–0.65: First fauna — simple nervous systems. Like insects: patterned, efficient, numerous.
type_of_life.ectotherm_fauna: 0.66–0.73: Cold-blooded mobile consumers. Like reptiles: fast when warm, complex data flows.
type_of_life.endotherm_fauna: 0.74–0.80: Warm-blooded, all 32 chromosomes active. Like mammals: self-regulating, rich organism ecosystem.
type_of_life.tribal: 0.81–0.86: CIVILIZATION BEGINS — CivilizationGenerator activates. Local state, basic routing, flat tokens. Like the first villages: shared resources, simple governance.
type_of_life.city_state: 0.87–0.91: Organised settlement. Shared context, protected routing, semantic tokens. Like Athens: governed by rules, commands obeyed.
type_of_life.nation_state: 0.92–0.94: Unified governance. Reactive store, platform routing, component tokens. Like nation-states: standardised systems.
type_of_life.empire: 0.95–0.96: Distributed authority. Persisted state, federated routing, governed tokens. Like Rome: decentralised but coherent.
type_of_life.network: 0.97–0.98: Post-geographic coordination. Federated state, Module Federation, cross-system tokens. Like the internet: protocol over geography.
type_of_life.singularity: 0.99–1.00: Convergence — all systems unified. Event bus + Module Federation + cross-system token graph. Like post-singularity intelligence: one living system.

definition_of_life.what_we_look_for: Not just visual existence, but functional fitness

metrics.adaptability: How well design responds to viewport/environment changes
metrics.resilience: Graceful degradation under stress (slow networks, reduced motion)
metrics.evolvability: Potential for mutation while maintaining function

definition_of_life.false_life: A template that looks alive but cannot adapt is not life—it's a statue. Permutations designs are living because they emerge from process, not prescription.

ecosystem_intricacies.description: No organism exists in isolation—components form ecosystems
ecosystem_intricacies.symbiosis: Hero section (ch19) and trust signals (ch21) co-evolve: high-trust sectors enhance hero credibility
ecosystem_intricacies.predator_prey: Motion (ch8) hunts attention—too much consumes usability. Constraints keep the food chain balanced.
ecosystem_intricacies.niche_partitioning: Typography (ch3/ch4) and color (ch5/ch6) occupy different niches but compete for dominance—epistasis resolves conflict
ecosystem_intricacies.keystone_species: Accessibility (ch17) is the keystone—remove it and the entire ecosystem collapses for disabled users
ecosystem_intricacies.trophic_levels: Energy (user attention) flows: hero → content → CTA. Grid (ch9) and hierarchy (ch10) define the energy pyramid.

evolution.mechanism: Mutation of seed → variation → selection by constraints

process.step_2: Chromosomes mutate within sector bounds (hue ±30°, not red→green in healthcare)
process.step_3: Constraint solver selects viable offspring (viability score > 0.7)
process.step_4: User selection drives next generation ('warmer colors', 'more density')

evolution.speciation: Dashboards and portfolios diverged from common ancestor (high information density) but adapted to different environments (scanning vs browsing)
evolution.convergent_evolution: Healthcare and banking both evolved trust signals independently—similar environmental pressure (user vulnerability) produces similar solutions

types.chromosomal_inversion: Swap topology (ch1) from flat to deep—major structural change
types.gene_duplication: Add trust signal variants (ch21) without removing existing
types.horizontal_transfer: Import hero type from different sector (automotive configurator into real estate)

mutation.selection_pressure: User intent applies pressure. 'More professional' = select for geometric fonts, cool colors, high contrast. 'More playful' = organic shapes, warm palette, spring physics.

advancement.thresholds: Complexity isn't linear—it phase-transitions at critical points

great_filters.0_34: From abiotic to life—first bryophyte containers emerge at this threshold
great_filters.0_81: From ecosystem to civilization—tribal tier activates CivilizationGenerator (state topology, routing, token inheritance)
great_filters.0_97: From empire to network—Module Federation routing and cross-system token graph required

technological_leaps.fire: Discovery of hero types—not all heroes need 3D
technological_leaps.industrial: Component library generation—mass production of variants
technological_leaps.space: Generative components—creating what is needed, not what is predefined

characteristics: Components generate on-demand based on content
Color palette adapts to user mood/time of day
Layout reconfigures for individual cognitive preferences
Motion responds to user's current task context
Accessibility anticipates needs before they're expressed
maximum_complexity.fermi_paradox: If advanced complexity is possible, why haven't we seen it? Because current AI tools default to mediocrity. Permutations is the SETI program for design—we're listening for signals of intelligent design systems.
maximum_complexity.drake_equation_for_design:
N = R* × fp × ne × fl × fi × fc × L
Where:
R* = rate of design requests
fp = fraction with viable seeds
ne = seeds per sector
fl = fraction that achieve life (viability > 0.7)
fi = fraction that achieve intelligence (complexity > 0.70)
fc = fraction that achieve civilization (complexity > 0.85)
L = lifetime of the design system

closing_thought:
We didn't build another design tool. We built a genetic framework for digital aesthetics. The 32 chromosomes are the letters of an alphabet. The SHA-256 hash is the seed. The constraints are the grammar. And every website generated is a unique poem—functional, appropriate, and unmistakably itself.

In the cosmic sense: We created a universe where design systems evolve, ecosystems thrive across 14 biological tiers, and civilization emerges from the mathematical substrate. Every seed is a world. Every genome is a living thing. And every designer using Permutations is an astronomer, discovering new planets in an infinite design space.
